avalancheActivity.highlights: In regions exposed to heavier precipitation and at intermediate and high altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and the danger is level 4 (high).
avalancheActivity.comment:
A very large quantity of fresh snow as well as the sometimes large wind slabs are prone to triggering in all aspects. The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released very easily. In these regions more large avalanches are possible.
As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase. This applies in particular on steep slopes especially at low and intermediate altitudes.

As before, it is inadvisable to engage in ski touring, freeriding and snowshoe hiking.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow.
The new snow has settled a little at low and intermediate altitudes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Plenty of new snow, wind-slab snow and rising temperatures below 2,700 metres; avalanche danger remains.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The large quantity of fresh snow as well as the wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2700 m. As the day progresses the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on very steep sunny slopes. In particular here natural avalanches, capable of reaching low altitudes, must be expected.

Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow can be released by a single winter sport participant. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.
Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 50 to 70 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Sunday above approximately 1500 m. The weather conditions fostered a settling of the snowpack.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes.
Especially steep shady slopes above the tree line: Weak layers exist in the top section of the snowpack here.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Plenty of new snow, wind-slab snow and rising temperatures below 2,700 metres; avalanche danger remains.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The large quantity of fresh snow as well as the wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2700 m. As the day progresses the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on very steep sunny slopes. In the typical avalanche paths in particular the wet avalanches can in isolated cases reach as far as the valley bottom.

Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 80 to 120 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Sunday above approximately 1500 m. The weather conditions fostered a settling of the snowpack.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes.
Especially steep shady slopes above the tree line: Weak layers exist in the top section of the snowpack here.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will decrease gradually.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh and older wind slabs are easy to recognise and to be assessed critically. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Fresh and older wind slabs are easy to recognise and can in some cases be released easily. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes. On very steep sunny slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. As the day progresses, wet and gliding avalanches are possible.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. On steep shady slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size.
The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. This applies in particular on wind-loaded slopes above the tree line.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 50 cm of snow fell on Sunday above approximately 2000 m. The fresh snow and the sometimes deep wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. Especially above approximately 2000 m northwest, north and northeast facing slopes:

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions up to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen. The wind was moderate to strong. The wind slabs of the last few days represent the main danger. They must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. As the day progresses the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually. Avalanches can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In all aspects the wind slabs have increased in size.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Light precipitation. The wind will be strong at times.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches and moist loose snow avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released easily, or, in isolated cases naturally and generally at intermediate and high altitudes. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released even by a single winter sport participant.
As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase. This applies in particular on steep slopes especially at low and intermediate altitudes.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. Over a wide area new snow is lying on a hard crust.
The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The covering of new snow is soft and its surface has a crust that is not capable of bearing a load.
The moderate wind has transported the new snow.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
A lot of snow fell in the last two days over a wide area. As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind, wind slabs formed in particular at intermediate and high altitudes.
The fresh wind slabs can be released easily or naturally. In particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and on wind-loaded slopes large avalanches are possible.

Snow sport activities outside marked and open pistes call for experience and great restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh wind slabs are easy to recognise and can in some cases be released easily especially at their margins. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh wind slabs are easy to recognise are to be assessed critically. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack on very steep sunny slopes.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. On steep shady slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.
The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities up to 25 cm of snow fell on Sunday. The fresh snow and the wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. Especially above approximately 2000 m northwest, north and northeast facing slopes:

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the sometimes storm force wind the wind slabs will increase in size moderately. The fresh wind slabs can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size.
Avalanches can in isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep northwest, north and southeast facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain above approximately 2000 m. The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase with altitude. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

In addition small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of a strong to storm force wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. They will be deposited on soft layers.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on northwest, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. The old snowpack will be moist.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches and wet avalanches during the day are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported a lot of snow. The fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
In addition as the day progresses at low and intermediate altitudes, moist and wet avalanches are to be expected.
Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for defensive route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow fell on Sunday above approximately 1500 m. The weather conditions facilitated a settling of the snowpack.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2700 m.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the extensive wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Very steep, little used shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies at high altitude.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good in some case. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released easily.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow and the sometimes deep wind slabs can be released easily in particular on northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach large size.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last two days above approximately 1700 m. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Below approximately 1800 m: The old snowpack remains moist.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the sometimes storm force wind the wind slabs will increase in size moderately. The fresh wind slabs can in some cases be released by a single winter sport participant, but they will be small in most cases.
Avalanches can in isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep northwest, north and southeast facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase with altitude.

In addition mostly small moist loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The wind will be strong to storm force at times. At elevated altitudes rather small wind slabs will form. They will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on near-ridge shady slopes at elevated altitudes.
The mostly small wind slabs of the last few days have settled a little.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on northwest, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2200 m.

Below approximately 2200 m and steep sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. The old snowpack will be moist.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities.
The wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released easily. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the sometimes storm force wind the wind slabs will increase in size moderately. The fresh wind slabs can in some cases be released by a single winter sport participant. Mostly avalanches are rather small.
Avalanches can in isolated cases be triggered in near-ground layers and reach medium size.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep northwest, north and southeast facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and larger.

In addition mostly small moist loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The wind will be strong to storm force at times. At elevated altitudes rather small wind slabs will form. They will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack in particular on near-ridge shady slopes at elevated altitudes.
The wind slabs of the last few days have settled a little.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on northwest, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2200 m.

Below approximately 2200 m and steep sunny slopes: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. The old snowpack will be moist.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding avalanches are the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack. These conditions brought about a gradual weakening of the snowpack also at intermediate and high altitudes. In some localities light snowfall above approximately 1300 m.

tendency.highlights: 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Wednesday above approximately 1300 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: New snow is lying on a moist old snowpack. Above approximately 1300 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.

avalancheActivity.comment:
On extremely steep slopes small moist and wet snow slides are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular.
Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes.
